🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 30 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 14 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. ai_workflow_automation“Here is a European B2B SaaS startup building AI agents on top of n8n, Make, or Zapier APIs to automate multi-step enterprise workflows.”
2. ai_workflow_automation“Here is a European AI startup adjacent to robotic process automation, applying LLMs to invoice matching, purchase order approval, or expense categorization.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 30 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 30 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — Profund VC
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 3 companies → evaluated: 1 for Profund VC

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.
LinkedIn Posts — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 2 leads

Apify LinkedIn Post Search → scans posts from the last 7 days (postedLimit=week, sortBy=date). Gemini Flash content miner reads each post and extracts company names from the text. 1 query/day per thesis, rotating through all queries.

Per-thesis | Rotation: test=2/day (1/thesis), prod=2/day (1/thesis) cycling through 8 queries | postedLimit: week | sortBy: date | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.10/run

LinkedIn Jobs — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (Profund VC)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 24 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads
Apify raw: 30 → no URL: -9 → passed to LLM: 24

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

Wego10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Wego is a B2C travel search site, not a B2B software product for professional or institutional buyers. 🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 307 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

THIS RUN — RESULTS: 7 evaluated (0 MATCH · 7 NO MATCH) · 132 dropped
CompanyScoreVerdictKey reasoningURLSource
Fulcrum Agent Rentals30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails. The description 'Your own AI agent' without further context implies a general-purpose AI tool, which often targets consumers or very broad B2B use cases, and lacks specificity of being a B2B SaaS product for professional or institutional buyers.https://www.launchingnext.com/s/fulcrum-agent-rent
Count10NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1.https://www.techstars.com
Cal.com Platform30NO MATCHCal.com Platform, as described, is a scheduling tool for individuals and teams, which aligns with B2C or general B2B productivity rather than the specific B2B AI workflow automation for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams as required by STEP B's 'ai_workflow_automation' angle. It also does not fit the 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' angle.https://cal.com/appv7_3
Remy AI30NO MATCHSTEP 1 of the 'ai_workflow_automation' angle requires the product to be sold to operations, finance, legal, or HR teams. Remy AI focuses on product teams and founders for ideation and design, which does not align with the specified target departments.https://www.withremy.comv7_3
Claude by Anthropic10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (Vertical/sector gate) fails: Claude by Anthropic provides foundational AI models, which are general-purpose AI capabilities, not a specific B2B SaaS product for workflow automation or regulated intermediaries. It lacks the vertical workflow focus required by the thesis.https://www.anthropic.com/learn/claude-for-workv7_3
Lovable10NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed because Lovable's platform for building websites and apps using natural language is a B2C productivity tool, not a B2B software product primarily sold to professional or institutional buyers, nor does it align with the specified AI workflow automation or regulated intermediary fintech angles. The product is described as enabling 'users to create digital projects' and primarily used by 'non-technical users' for 'early-stage prototypes', indicating a consumer or prosumer focus.https://alitoday.com/lovable-a-swedish-ai-startup-v7_3
Welltory30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails. Welltory's core business, as described, is a B2B employee wellness and productivity app, not an AI workflow automation tool for operations, finance, legal, or HR, nor a regulated-intermediary fintech. While it targets companies ('B2B'), its function is employee well-being enhancement, which does not align with the specified angles.https://wbfl.welltory.com/for-companiesv7_3

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

Shared | Rotation: NONE — all NAF codes daily (90-day lookback) | Cost: Pappers subscription

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.
GitHub — Developer Repos (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Searches GitHub repos and organizations for projects matching merged sector keywords. Finds technical founders building in your space before they raise. Free GitHub API, runs daily.

Shared | Rotation: NONE | Cost: $0.00 (free) | Test: 30 leads | Prod: 30 leads

yschimke20NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. The company provides tools to render UI previews for AI coding agents, which is a developer tool, not a B2B software product sold to professional or institutional buyers outside of a developer context or an AI workflow product for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams.https://yschimke.github.io/compose-ai-tools/
yschimke10NO MATCHThe company provides tools for rendering UI components for AI coding agents, which is unrelated to corporate travel technology. It does not fit any of the specified angles (hotel booking, TMC workflow, payments, or ERP integrations) within the travel domain. Therefore, it fails STEP 1.https://yschimke.github.io/compose-ai-tools/
yschimke10NO MATCHCompany develops AI tools for rendering UI components, which does not align with physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, telecare/personal emergency response, or SOC-platform technology (STEP A).https://yschimke.github.io/compose-ai-tools/
RLinf10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fail: The company provides 'Reinforcement Learning Infrastructure for Embodied and Agentic AI', which appears to be developer tooling or research infrastructure for AI, not a B2B SaaS workflow automation product or regulated-intermediary fintech.https://rlinf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
RLinf10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because the company's core business is in Reinforcement Learning infrastructure for Embodied and Agentic AI, which is entirely unrelated to corporate travel technology. It is not B2B software, SaaS, or tech-enabled services for hotel booking, travel policy, payments, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://rlinf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
RLinf0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (Company core business) fails. CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

sforbt
1 MATCH · 65 NO MATCH · 2% hit rate
🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 30 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. hotel_booking_saas“Here is a European travel tech startup integrating AI-powered hotel inventory aggregation with real-time policy enforcement and duty of care alerts for business travelers.”
2. hotel_booking_saas“Here is a European startup in corporate extended-stay or group lodging procurement, adjacent to transient hotel booking for enterprise clients with high-frequency travel.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.
QUERY POOL — Full Rotation Cycle (28-day full cycle · test: 1/day per thesis · 28 total):
hotel_booking_saas weight=5 — 7 entries
in 26d — Wed Jun 17“Here is a European B2B SaaS platform automating corporate hotel booking and preferred hotel program management for enterprise travel managers, replacing manual GDS searches.”
in 27d — Thu Jun 18“Here is a hotel booking technology sold to Procurement Officers and CFOs in European multinationals seeking centralized lodging spend visibility and policy compliance.”
★ TODAY“Here is a French or German startup providing a corporate hotel marketplace with negotiated rates and automated booking for mid-market firms with significant travel budgets.”
in 1d — Sat May 23“Here is a Here is an early-stage European SaaS company at Seed or Series A building hotel sourcing and RFP management for travel procurement teams in professional services firms.”
in 2d — Sun May 24“Here is a European travel tech startup integrating AI-powered hotel inventory aggregation with real-time policy enforcement and duty of care alerts for business travelers.”
in 3d — Mon May 25“Here is a European startup in corporate extended-stay or group lodging procurement, adjacent to transient hotel booking for enterprise clients with high-frequency travel.”
in 4d — Tue May 26“Here is a European corporate hotel booking platform similar to HRS Group or Roomex, targeting mid-market and enterprise buyers needing multi-property hotel consolidation.”
tmc_workflow_platform weight=4 — 7 entries
in 5d — Wed May 27“Here is a European SaaS platform automating TMC mid-office workflow including ticketing, queue management, and booking servicing for travel management companies.”
in 6d — Thu May 28“Here is a workflow automation tool sold to TMC operations directors and travel agency team leads in Europe to reduce manual agent workload per transaction.”
in 7d — Fri May 29“Here is a French or UK startup providing back-office automation software for travel agencies handling corporate accounts and multi-GDS booking queues.”
in 8d — Sat May 30“Here is a Seed or Series A European company building mid-office automation that enables small TMCs to compete with Amex GBT and CWT on servicing costs.”
in 9d — Sun May 31“Here is a European startup using robotic process automation or AI to handle GDS scripting, PNR management, and exception handling for travel management companies.”
in 10d — Mon Jun 1“Here is a European startup adjacent to TMC workflow in corporate meetings management, group travel operations, or event travel logistics automation for enterprises.”
in 11d — Tue Jun 2“Here is a European TMC workflow automation platform similar to Travelport or KDS, targeting travel management companies needing agent productivity and booking servicing software.”
corporate_travel_payments weight=4 — 7 entries
in 12d — Wed Jun 3“Here is a European B2B SaaS or fintech platform automating corporate hotel invoice capture and cross-border VAT recovery for finance directors managing large travel budgets.”
in 13d — Thu Jun 4“Here is a virtual card issuance and lodging payment reconciliation tool sold to CFOs and treasury teams in European multinationals to eliminate manual hotel invoice processing.”
in 14d — Fri Jun 5“Here is a French or German startup providing AI-powered hotel folio extraction and EU VAT reclaim automation for corporate travel finance teams.”
in 15d — Sat Jun 6“Here is a Here is an early-stage European fintech company at Seed or Series A automating PCI-compliant hotel payment workflows integrated with SAP or Oracle ERP systems.”
in 16d — Sun Jun 7“Here is a European startup using machine learning for multi-currency hotel invoice reconciliation and automatic GL coding into enterprise accounting systems for travel spend.”
in 17d — Mon Jun 8“Here is a European company adjacent to travel payments in corporate card management, spend analytics for lodging, or procurement payment orchestration for business travel programs.”
in 18d — Tue Jun 9“Here is a European corporate travel payment platform similar to AirPlus or Conferma Pay, targeting finance directors needing automated hotel payment and VAT recovery.”
travel_erp_integrations weight=3 — 7 entries
in 19d — Wed Jun 10“Here is a European B2B SaaS company building ERP integration middleware connecting corporate travel booking systems to SAP or Oracle for automated GL coding and invoice posting.”
in 20d — Thu Jun 11“Here is a data integration platform sold to IT Directors and Finance Managers in European enterprises to eliminate manual re-keying between TMC back-office and accounting systems.”
in 21d — Fri Jun 12“Here is a French or German startup providing a travel-to-ERP data connector that automates hotel invoice coding and booking data synchronization for mid-market European companies.”
in 22d — Sat Jun 13“Here is a Seed-stage European SaaS startup building middleware to integrate hotel booking data with corporate finance systems and automate travel spend reconciliation workflows.”
in 23d — Sun Jun 14“Here is a European startup using API-first architecture to connect TMC back-office data feeds with enterprise ERP systems including multi-currency hotel invoice classification.”
in 24d — Mon Jun 15“Here is a European startup adjacent to travel ERP integration providing NDC content aggregation middleware, travel data warehousing, or booking analytics infrastructure for enterprises.”
in 25d — Tue Jun 16“Here is a European travel back-office integration platform similar to SIAP or Datalex, selling ERP connectors and data middleware to TMCs and corporate travel departments.”
THIS RUN — RESULTS: 10 evaluated (1 MATCH · 9 NO MATCH) · 20 dropped
CompanyScoreVerdictKey reasoningURL
FlowMate10NO MATCHSTEP 1: FlowMate is a generic embedded automation OS for SaaS companies, AI platforms, and workflow builders. It is not specifically focused on European corporate travel technology (hotel booking, travel policy, payments, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel).https://flowmate.io
Rossum15NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Rossum provides AI-powered intelligent document processing, which is a generic automation tool for transactional workflows across various industries, not specifically focused on European corporate travel technology. It does not align with hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://rossum.ai

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 60 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 60 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — sforbt
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 3 companies → evaluated: 1 for sforbt

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.

LinkedIn Jobs — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (sforbt)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 24 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads
Apify raw: 30 → no URL: -9 → passed to LLM: 24

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run
THIS RUN — 1 entry for sforbt:
Week 21 → angle: hotel_booking_saasBarcelona | Business Travel | 11-50 employees
ⓘ operatingStatus=Active · companyType=For Profit · Country auto-filled from city.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

Wego10NO MATCHThe company, Wego, operates as a travel search site primarily focused on the Asia Pacific and Middle East, indicating a consumer-facing leisure travel product, which fails STEP 1 of the gate question (European corporate travel technology domain).https://e27.co/startups/wegoskift.com/feed
🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 307 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

CompanyScoreVerdictKey reasoningURLSource
Fulcrum Agent Rentals0NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — The company description 'Your own AI agent' is too vague and does not provide enough information to determine if it operates within the European corporate travel technology domain or aligns with any of the specified angles. It fails STEP 1.https://www.launchingnext.com/s/fulcrum-agent-rent
Count0NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1. The provided description is for Techstars, an accelerator, and gives no information about the company 'Count'.https://www.techstars.com
Cal.com Platform10NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. Cal.com provides a general scheduling platform, not a solution for corporate travel booking, payment, TMC workflow, or ERP integration.https://cal.com/appv7_3
Remy AI10NO MATCHRemy AI focuses on AI ideation agents for founders and product teams to visualize product possibilities, not on corporate travel technology, hotel booking, TMC workflow, corporate travel payments, or ERP integrations for travel.https://www.withremy.comv7_3
Claude by Anthropic10NO MATCHClaude by Anthropic is a foundational AI model. While it has various B2B applications, including for engineering, HR, marketing, product management, and sales, its core business does not fall within the European corporate travel technology domain. Therefore, it fails STEP A of the gate question.https://www.anthropic.com/learn/claude-for-workv7_3
Lovable10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails: Lovable's core business is an AI-powered platform for building websites and apps using natural language, not corporate travel technology. It is a generic dev tool, not travel-specific infrastructure.https://alitoday.com/lovable-a-swedish-ai-startup-v7_3
Welltory10NO MATCHThe company, Welltory, provides an AI-powered wellness app for employee wellbeing and stress reduction, which is explicitly a B2B HR tool. This does not align with any of the specified European corporate travel technology verticals outlined in STEP 1, such as hotel booking, payments, or TMC workflow. The company's core business is employee wellness, not business travel.https://wbfl.welltory.com/for-companiesv7_3

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

Shared | Rotation: NONE — all NAF codes daily (90-day lookback) | Cost: Pappers subscription

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.
GitHub — Developer Repos (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Searches GitHub repos and organizations for projects matching merged sector keywords. Finds technical founders building in your space before they raise. Free GitHub API, runs daily.

Shared | Rotation: NONE | Cost: $0.00 (free) | Test: 30 leads | Prod: 30 leads

yschimke20NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. The company provides tools to render UI previews for AI coding agents, which is a developer tool, not a B2B software product sold to professional or institutional buyers outside of a developer context or an AI workflow product for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams.https://yschimke.github.io/compose-ai-tools/
yschimke10NO MATCHThe company provides tools for rendering UI components for AI coding agents, which is unrelated to corporate travel technology. Therefore, it fails STEP 1.https://yschimke.github.io/compose-ai-tools/
yschimke10NO MATCHCompany develops AI tools for rendering UI components, which does not align with physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, telecare/personal emergency response, or SOC-platform technology (STEP A).https://yschimke.github.io/compose-ai-tools/
RLinf10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fail: The company provides 'Reinforcement Learning Infrastructure for Embodied and Agentic AI', which appears to be developer tooling or research infrastructure for AI, not a B2B SaaS workflow automation product or regulated-intermediary fintech.https://rlinf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
RLinf10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because the company's core business is in Reinforcement Learning infrastructure for Embodied and Agentic AI, which is entirely unrelated to corporate travel technology. It is not B2B software, SaaS, or tech-enabled services for hotel booking, travel policy, payments, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://rlinf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
RLinf0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (Company core business) fails. RLinf provides 'Reinforcement Learning Infrastructure for Embodied and Agentic AI', which is a software framework for AI development, not physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, telecare, or SOC-platform technology for such services. It does not align with any of the specified verticals.https://rlinf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
sjkim112710NO MATCHSTEP 1: The company provides an MCP server for automated reverse engineering, which does not align with 'AI-native workflow automation product sold to operations, finance, legal, or HR teams' nor 'B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries'.https://github.com/sjkim1127/Reversecore_MCP

sjkim112710NO MATCHThe company provides an AI-powered server for automated reverse engineering, which is cybersecurity-related and not physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, or telecare services. CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

NEXECUR
5 MATCH · 79 NO MATCH · 6% hit rate
🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 30 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for NEXECUR (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. residential_monitoring_consolidation“Here is a connected home alarm platform company in France providing residential intrusion detection with remote arming via mobile app and 24/7 monitoring center.”
2. residential_monitoring_consolidation“Here is a Belgian or Swiss French-speaking residential alarm monitoring operator with contracted subscriber base and certified monitoring infrastructure seeking acquisition.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 80 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 80 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 4 seeds for NEXECUR (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Sector Alarm France (anchor)
2. Honeywell Security France (anchor)
3. Présence Verte (anchor)
4. Patriot Systems (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

CompanyScoreVerdictKey reasoningURL
Lafrancaisedesecurite90MATCHThe company's core business is physical premises security in France (STEP A and C), and it aligns with the 'smb_security_expansion' angle by serving professional clients and high-value sites with installation, maintenance, and remote monitoring (STEP B and D).https://lafrancaisedesecurite.fr
Vedis25NO MATCHVedis is a security systems integrator, installing and maintaining security solutions. While they operate in France and serve B2B customers, the description indicates they are a service provider that designs, advises, and installs electronic security solutions, not an alarm monitoring operator with a contracted subscriber base or a telecare provider per the active angles. They do not appear to have an in-house certified monitoring center or offer subscription-based remote monitoring services, which is a core component of the thesis angles. Therefore, it fails STEP B, as it primarily offers installation and integration services instead of monitoring services.https://vedis.fr
Devauze90MATCHDevauze specializes in security solutions for residential and commercial buildings, including alarm systems and 7/7 security services in France, indicating physical premises security operations (STEP A). It serves both residential and commercial clients (matching residential_monitoring_consolidation and smb_security_expansion angles for STEP B), and is headquartered in France (STEP C). The company has operated since 1972, suggesting established revenue and an existing subscriber base (STEP D).https://devauze.com
Optex Europe20NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Optex Europe specializes in detection technologies and security solutions (perimeter protection, access control, automatic doors, infrared/laser detection, video surveillance integrations) sold to businesses for physical premises security, not remote alarm monitoring, telecare, or SOC platform technology as required by the thesis.https://optex-europe.com
Urmetgroup10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because while Urmetgroup provides physical security solutions and components, the description does not indicate they are an alarm monitoring operator (télésurveillance) or telecare provider themselves. They design and manufacture hardware and systems for access control, interphony, video surveillance, and home automation, but act more as suppliers to professional partners rather than direct monitoring service providers with a subscriber base, which is inconsistent with the core thesis.https://urmetgroup.fr
Alceaglobal10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails. While Alceaglobal provides physical premises security, its focus is on critical infrastructure, not remote alarm monitoring, telecare/personal-emergency response, or SOC-platform technology for residential/SMB/elderly as required by the thesis. It does not align with any of the defined angles.https://alceaglobal.com
S Genelec30NO MATCHS Genelec is a security systems integrator, not an alarm monitoring operator, telecare provider, or SOC platform. They install and maintain security systems but do not appear to offer monitoring services.https://s-genelec.fr
Amper Asso10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails. Amper Asso provides general home care and assistance services, such as housekeeping and meal delivery, which are not physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, or SOC-platform technology. While they offer 'teleassistance', the overall description points to a broad range of social and personal support services rather than a dedicated telecare operator as defined by the thesis.https://amper-asso.fr
Msa Services25NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. The company's core business is social and solidarity services, including HR, IT, and home care. While it mentions 'teleassistance', it is broadly applied and not specifically focused on physical premises security or remote alarm monitoring as defined by the thesis. It is not a telecare/personal-emergency response operator or SOC-platform technology provider.https://msa-services.fr
Presenceverte95MATCHThe company's core business is telecare/personal-emergency response in France, directly matching the 'telecare_aging_population' angle. It serves elderly and persons with reduced mobility, offers 24/7 monitoring-related services, and has an established subscriber base.https://presenceverte.fr
Presenceverte So90MATCHThe company, Présence Verte Sud-Ouest, is a French nonprofit telecare operator serving the elderly with 24/7 teleassistance (personal emergency response) under a monthly subscription model, fitting the 'telecare_aging_population' angle (STEP B) and based in France (STEP C), and appears to have established operations (STEP D).https://presenceverte-so.fr
Patriotalarm0NO MATCHSTEP 1 fails because Patriot Alarms is a US-based manufacturer of fire protection and life-safety systems, and not operating in France or contiguous European markets as required. Furthermore, the company does not align with any of the specified acquisition angles, which are all French-market focused. The company's primary operations are in the US, thus failing STEP C as well. Also the company is not an alarm monitoring company.https://patriotalarm.com
Mtiss0NO MATCHSTEP A fails as the company is a physical security and investigations firm focusing on government and commercial critical infrastructure, not specifically remote alarm monitoring or telecare, and does not operate in France or contiguous European markets. STEP C further fails due to US headquarters.https://mtiss.com
Patriotsecurityinc0NO MATCHThe company operates in security services but is based in the US (Texas and Louisiana), failing STEP C which requires operations or HQ in France, Belgium, or French-speaking Switzerland.https://patriotsecurityinc.com
Sentrysix0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails because the company provides security and investigations services, including surveillance and AI intrusion detection, primarily to government clients in the US, which does not align with the thesis's focus on physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, or telecare services specifically in France or contiguous European markets.https://sentrysix.com
Dmtradar0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails. Dmtradar provides turn-key security and surveillance radar solutions for high-security applications like ports and airports, which is not physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, or telecare/personal-emergency response in the required context.https://dmtradar.com

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — NEXECUR
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 3 companies → evaluated: 1 for NEXECUR

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.

↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 2 leads

Apify LinkedIn Post Search → scans posts from the last 7 days (postedLimit=week, sortBy=date). Gemini Flash content miner reads each post and extracts company names from the text. 1 query/day per thesis, rotating through all queries.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (NEXECUR)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 24 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads
Apify raw: 30 → no URL: -9 → passed to LLM: 24

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 307 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

Shared | Rotation: NONE — all NAF codes daily (90-day lookback) | Cost: Pappers subscription

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.
GitHub — Developer Repos (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Searches GitHub repos and organizations for projects matching merged sector keywords. Finds technical founders building in your space before they raise. Free GitHub API, runs daily.

Shared | Rotation: NONE | Cost: $0.00 (free) | Test: 30 leads | Prod: 30 leads

yschimke20NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. The company provides tools to render UI previews for AI coding agents, which is a developer tool, not a B2B software product sold to professional or institutional buyers outside of a developer context or an AI workflow product for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams.https://yschimke.github.io/compose-ai-tools/
yschimke10NO MATCHThe company provides tools for rendering UI components for AI coding agents, which is unrelated to corporate travel technology. Therefore, it fails STEP 1.https://yschimke.github.io/compose-ai-tools/
yschimke10NO MATCHCompany develops AI tools for rendering UI components, which does not align with physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, telecare/personal emergency response, or SOC-platform technology (STEP A).https://yschimke.github.io/compose-ai-tools/
RLinf10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fail: The company provides 'Reinforcement Learning Infrastructure for Embodied and Agentic AI', which appears to be developer tooling or research infrastructure for AI, not a B2B SaaS workflow automation product or regulated-intermediary fintech.https://rlinf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
RLinf10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because the company's core business is in Reinforcement Learning infrastructure for Embodied and Agentic AI, which is entirely unrelated to corporate travel technology. It is not B2B software, SaaS, or tech-enabled services for hotel booking, travel policy, payments, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://rlinf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
RLinf0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (Company core business) fails. RLinf provides 'Reinforcement Learning Infrastructure for Embodied and Agentic AI', which is a software framework for AI development, not physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, telecare, or SOC-platform technology for such services. CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.
